results.title: Timboon Consolidated School
results.creator_credit: E.J. Perry and Victoria. Dept. of Education and Early Childhood Development
results.brief_description: Makes an argument for the consolidation of local small rural schools into large purpose built centralised establishments with a post-primary school element. Presents the organisational elements involved: bussing, staffing, design, curriculum etc.

results.title: Oranges and lemons
results.creator_credit: Margot Lethlean and Swinburne Institute of Technology. Film and Television School
results.brief_description: A documentary on the early education of aboriginal children. It looks at a special pre-school program to help prepare aboriginal children for school, which has been set up in Dareton, Victoria. It suggests that in the face of insensitive attitudes, conventional education will further undermine aboriginal culture.

results.title: Skin deep
results.creator_credit: Bill Veitch, Norman Laird and Tasmanian Government Film Unit
results.brief_description: Illustrates the work of the Peter McCallum Clinic in Launceston, Tasmania, with particular reference to the treatment of cancer by X-ray machine and cobalt bomb. The central character is a patient with early cancer who finds that early treatment leads to cure.

results.title: One of the family
results.creator_credit: Bernice Rubens, Derrick Knight and Derrick Knight and Partners
results.brief_description: An assessment of handicapped children which concludes that the frustration of the child is usually less than that of the parents; but with understanding, both parents and children alike can come to terms with their problems.

results.title: Remembering Janefield ...
results.creator_credit: Eastern Energy and Land Use Team, State of Victoria, Department of Human Services and Tribal Communications
results.brief_description: Provides a brief history of the Janefield Training Centre, Bundoora with both ex-residents and ex-staff members sharing anecdotes and moving reflections on their experiences and years at the institution.

results.title: Cornflakes for tea
results.creator_credit: John Colquhoun, Tom Broadbridge and Andromeda Productions
results.brief_description: Dramatization of how a family living in high-rise inner city flats manage to cope with caring for each other when first their mother and then their father abandon them. Provides many points which could act as stimulus for discussion including perspectives on family relationships, contrasts on single parent and conventional nuclear families, welfare workers and their role, supportive community members, and the warm relationship of the central characters.
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Colquhoun, Tom Broadbridge and Andromeda Productions

results.title: Visitors in time
results.brief_description: Explores the way of life of the inhabitants of the former Portuguese island of Timor in the Malay archipelago. Shows the various ways in which these islanders earn a living, highlights the similarities between an ancient people and their present day descendants.

results.title: Something creative
results.creator_credit: Gillian Leahy and Crafts Council of Australia
results.brief_description: This film looks at the question of creativity in everyday life and examines the role of the professional artist in stimulating and encouraging participation in the arts by the community at large. Focuses on Orange and Blacktown in New South Wales. Cinematography by Erika Addis.

results.title: Surviving the Outback Rivers
results.brief_description: The vast Australian Outback has been described as a land of heroes and dead men. Join Scott Lambert and seven Australian army commandoes who are all recipients of the green beret on a three week rafting expedition, where enough provisions for one week have been taken, with the idea of “living off the land” for most of the time. Setting out from Darwin, the journey will take these adventurers down the Katherine River through the Katherine Gorge, then back through the Clarevale Crossing and the Daly River Crossing, back to Darwin. A fascinating adventure amongst some of the most beautiful wilderness in Australia.

results.title: Would you like gravy with that?
results.brief_description:
A short created by a student for the 2004 ACMI Diegesis secondary school media festival.
This work depicts the views of students, teachers and the filmmakers on the state of the De La Salle College canteen. The owner of the canteen is interviewed, as well as students and teachers who use the canteen.
